summary: Both round-1 advisories are correctly addressed. T2-SEC-01: DEPLOYER_KEY placeholder is now a non-parseable sentinel string that forge will reject at invocation time, eliminating the silent zero-key footgun. T2-SEC-02: forge-std submodule now has branch = v1.16.1 annotation in .gitmodules, at parity with the OZ entry. No new findings introduced by this fix commit. Task 2 is cleared for merge.

round1_findings_resolution.fix: contracts/.env.example: DEPLOYER_KEY changed from `0x000...000` (valid zero key) to `0x<your-0x-prefixed-64-hex-char-private-key>`. The angle-bracket sentinel is not valid hex; forge will fail to parse it and abort with an error before any transaction is broadcast. The comment above the key also now explicitly warns that the all-zeros key is a publicly-known monitored address and must not be used.
round1_findings_resolution.verification: Confirmed by reviewing diff in commit d5c5dc7. The new placeholder contains `<` and `>` characters which are not valid in a 0x-prefixed hex string — any tool that attempts to parse it as a private key will fail fast.

round1_findings_resolution.fix: .gitmodules: added `branch = v1.16.1` to the forge-std submodule entry. Now both OZ and forge-std entries carry a branch annotation for documentation parity. The cryptographic pin (commit SHA 620536fa... in the git index, corroborated by foundry.lock) is unchanged.
round1_findings_resolution.verification: Confirmed by reviewing diff in commit d5c5dc7. .gitmodules forge-std block now reads: path, url, branch = v1.16.1.

additional_observation.title: foundry.toml comment now documents foundry.lock purpose
additional_observation.detail: The fix commit also adds a comment in foundry.toml explaining that foundry.lock is a forge 1.6+ secondary lock that complements submodule SHA pinning. This is a positive addition: it clarifies that both the submodule SHA and foundry.lock are load-bearing for reproducibility, reducing the risk of a future contributor deleting foundry.lock thinking it is a generated artifact that need not be committed. No action required.

verdict.recommendation: APPROVE — all round-1 findings resolved, no new findings. Task 2 (Foundry setup) is secure and ready for merge.
